Rotten information board
 === Transportation === * Take the Kanachu Bus Fuji 34 from the north exit of Fujisawa Station or the west exit of Shonandai Station, or the Kanachu Bus Zen 03 from the west exit of Yoshiyuki Station, get off at "Benten-mae" and walk 6 minutes. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=545&cd=1206026&scd=&npg=0 Kumano Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Agency) === Notes === Currently, {{ill|lt=Kodai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|Q11675783|WD=Q11675783|lt=Kodai Jingu}} is in charge of managing both shrines, so you will need to get the {{ill|lt=Red seal (shrines and temples)|Q7504772|WD=Q7504772|lt=Goshuin}} from there. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Annotations"}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * From the precincts information board of Oba Shrine and Kumano Shrine === Related items === {{Commonscat|Ooba-jinja (Shikinaisya)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Sagami country|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|Q11588941|WD=Q11588941}} * {{ill|lt=Mikuriya Oba|Q11435381|WD=Q11435381}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ohashashiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:相模国の式内社]] [[Category:大庭氏|社]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}Inari997 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|scale:5000}} |Enshrined deity = God of the Emperor |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Tenjin-sama |Regular festival = September 15th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Oba Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}'''Oba Shrine'' (Oba Shrine) is {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} {{jalink|Sagami Province|Q105 described in "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) 3732}} is one (small shrine) of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|lt=13 Shikinaisha in Engi|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}}, but there are two {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (Hijosha). The distance between the two companies is relatively close at approximately 1.5{{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|lt=km|1=Kilometre}} (about 20 minutes walk). Both companies are listed in this section. === Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City) === '''Oba Shrine'' is located near the border with {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Inari {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. It is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} that is enshrined in . Located on {{ill|lt=hills|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} along {{ill|lt=Hikiji River|Q11487144|WD=Q11487144|1=Hikiji River}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Tenjingu," "Oba Daimyojin," and "Oba Tenmangu." Oba Shrine] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine). === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinnobureishin|1=Kamimusubi}}''' ({{ill|lt=Enshrined Deity#Main Deity/Augami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deity|1=Saijin}}) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Kagechika Oba|Q1155414|WD=Q1155414|lt=Kagechika Saburo Oba|1=Ōba Kagechika}} === History === Although "Engishiki Jinmyocho" compiled in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} mentions "{{ill|lt=Koza District|Q1202776|WD=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}} Oba Shrine," there are no other records (shaden) prior to {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}. The information board in the precincts about our company's history says, ``It was ranked as one of the 13 Sagami shrines and is a small shrine, and this place has been passed down as an old place since ancient times.'' "Sagami 13-no-Ichi Nishite-kosha 2 rows of Serarere are the old folklore of ancient places". The {{ill|lt=Bōshu bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} in the temple grounds was built in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}}6 ({{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}}). alink|casting|Q496098}}, and it is said that the words ``Tenmatenjingu'' were originally written on it, but these words were scratched off and the words ``Oba Daimyojin'' were carved into it. Also, according to "Fujisawa Cultural Properties" published by the {{ill|lt=Board of Education|Q399541|WD=Q399541|1=Board of education}} of Fujisawa City, our company was called "Tenjin Shrine" before {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}[http://pub.ne.jp/karihomaru/?entry_id=3609822 Two Oba Shrines] (Shonan Mark's room).{{ill|lt=Yasunaga (era name)|Q1193910|WD=Q1193910|lt=Yasunaga|1=An'ei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1777|Q7709|WD=Q7709|1=1777}}), King Shien of Jingi acquired Saburo Kagechika Oba, who was based in {{ill|lt=Oba Castle|Q11435376|WD=Q11435376|1=Oba Castle}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenmei|1=UNKNOWN}}3 ({{ill|lt=1783|Q7745|WD=Q7745|1=1783}}), Michima Sugawara was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}) according to the wishes of Suwabe Sadaro, Yamazaki Rokurobei Kanataka, and others. Before the Meiji era {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ordinance on the Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Jōjuin (Fujisawa City)|Q18337056|WD=Q18337056|lt=Jōjuin|1=Jōjuin}}, located behind our company, was managing our company as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. File:Ōba-jinja 01.jpg|

~Shonan Fujisawa Film Commission |publisher=Fujisawa City |accessdate=2018-11-10}}. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=543&cd=1206024&scd=&npg=0 Oba Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) === Kumano Shrine (Oba, Fujisawa City) === {{Shrine |Name = Kumano Shrine |Image= [[File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma03.JPG|250px]]

 === Transportation === * Take the Kanachu Bus Fuji 34 from the north exit of Fujisawa Station or the west exit of Shonandai Station, or the Kanachu Bus Zen 03 from the west exit of Yoshiyuki Station, get off at "Benten-mae" and walk 6 minutes. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=545&cd=1206026&scd=&npg=0 Kumano Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Agency) === Notes === Currently, {{ill|lt=Kodai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|Q11675783|WD=Q11675783|lt=Kodai Jingu|1=Kōdai-jingū}} is in charge of managing both shrines, so you will need to get the {{ill|lt=Red seal (shrines and temples)|Q7504772|WD=Q7504772|lt=Goshuin|1=Shuin}} from there. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Annotations"}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * From the precincts information board of Oba Shrine and Kumano Shrine === Related items === {{Commonscat|Ooba-jinja (Shikinaisya)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Sagami country|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|Q11588941|WD=Q11588941|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mikuriya Oba|Q11435381|WD=Q11435381|1=Mikuriya Oba}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ohashashiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:相模国の式内社]] [[Category:大庭氏|社]] [[Category:pages]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '神奈川県藤沢市稲荷997', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ooba-jinja (Shrine)}} [[JA:大庭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11435394}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oba Shrine |Image= [[File:OOBA-Jinjya-ten.JPG|250px]]
Oratory
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}Inari997 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|scale:5000}} |Enshrined deity = God of the Emperor |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Tenjin-sama |Regular festival = September 15th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Oba Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}'''Oba Shrine'' (Oba Shrine) is {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} {{jalink|Sagami Province|Q105 described in "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) 3732}} is one (small shrine) of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|lt=13 Shikinaisha in Engi|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}}, but there are two {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (Hijosha). The distance between the two companies is relatively close at approximately 1.5{{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|lt=km|1=Kilometre}} (about 20 minutes walk). Both companies are listed in this section. === Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City) === '''Oba Shrine'' is located near the border with {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Inari {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. It is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} that is enshrined in . Located on {{ill|lt=hills|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} along {{ill|lt=Hikiji River|Q11487144|WD=Q11487144|1=Hikiji River}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Tenjingu," "Oba Daimyojin," and "Oba Tenmangu." Oba Shrine] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine). === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinnobureishin|1=Kamimusubi}}''' ({{ill|lt=Enshrined Deity#Main Deity/Augami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deity|1=Saijin}}) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Kagechika Oba|Q1155414|WD=Q1155414|lt=Kagechika Saburo Oba|1=Ōba Kagechika}} === History === Although "Engishiki Jinmyocho" compiled in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} mentions "{{ill|lt=Koza District|Q1202776|WD=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}} Oba Shrine," there are no other records (shaden) prior to {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}. The information board in the precincts about our company's history says, ``It was ranked as one of the 13 Sagami shrines and is a small shrine, and this place has been passed down as an old place since ancient times.'' "Sagami 13-no-Ichi Nishite-kosha 2 rows of Serarere are the old folklore of ancient places". The {{ill|lt=Bōshu bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} in the temple grounds was built in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}}6 ({{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}}). alink|casting|Q496098}}, and it is said that the words ``Tenmatenjingu'' were originally written on it, but these words were scratched off and the words ``Oba Daimyojin'' were carved into it. Also, according to "Fujisawa Cultural Properties" published by the {{ill|lt=Board of Education|Q399541|WD=Q399541|1=Board of education}} of Fujisawa City, our company was called "Tenjin Shrine" before {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}[http://pub.ne.jp/karihomaru/?entry_id=3609822 Two Oba Shrines] (Shonan Mark's room).{{ill|lt=Yasunaga (era name)|Q1193910|WD=Q1193910|lt=Yasunaga|1=An'ei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1777|Q7709|WD=Q7709|1=1777}}), King Shien of Jingi acquired Saburo Kagechika Oba, who was based in {{ill|lt=Oba Castle|Q11435376|WD=Q11435376|1=Oba Castle}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenmei|1=UNKNOWN}}3 ({{ill|lt=1783|Q7745|WD=Q7745|1=1783}}), Michima Sugawara was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}) according to the wishes of Suwabe Sadaro, Yamazaki Rokurobei Kanataka, and others. Before the Meiji era {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ordinance on the Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Jōjuin (Fujisawa City)|Q18337056|WD=Q18337056|lt=Jōjuin|1=Jōjuin}}, located behind our company, was managing our company as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. File:Ōba-jinja 01.jpg|
{{ill|lt=Oratory|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} (July 2010)
File:OOBA-jinjya-ten02.JPG|
Temple bell
File:OOBA-jinjya-ten03.JPG|{{ill|lt=Great Kanto Earthquake|Q3742341|WD=Q3742341|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake}} Remains of {{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} === Transportation === {{osm box|n|1229000716|Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City)}} * From {{ill|lt=Fujisawa Station|Q1133110|WD=Q1133110|1=Fujisawa Station}} North Exit/{{ill|lt=Shonandai Station|Q1036357|WD=Q1036357|1=Shōnandai Station}} West Exit {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|Q10854960|WD=Q10854960|lt=Kanachu Bus|1=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu}}{{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu Fujisawa Office #Fujisawa Station North Exit - Ishikawa Bridge - Isshikigami - From the west exit of Shonandai Station|Q11588895|WD=Q11588895|lt=Fuji 34|1=Fuji 34}}, or from the west exit of Zengyo Station, take the Kanachu bus {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu Fujisawa Office #Zengyo Station - Wholesale Market Front|Q11588895|WD=Q11588895|lt=Zen 03|1=Zen 03}}, get off at Tenjinsha-mae, and walk 6 minutes to Ichi-no-Torii. * About 25 minutes walk from Odakyu Line {{ill|lt=Zengyo Station|Q4160904|WD=Q4160904|1=Zengyō Station}}. === A work set at Oba Shrine === * {{ill|lt=Futatsu no Spica|Q2511557|WD=Q2511557|1=Twin Spica}} - In {{ill|lt=2009|Q1996|WD=Q1996|1=2009}}, location shooting for the TV drama version that was broadcast in the "{{ill|lt=Drama 8|Q11323102|WD=Q11323102|1=Drama 8}}" slot of {{ill|lt=NHK General Television|Q909336|WD=Q909336|lt=NHK General Television|1=NHK General TV}} was held{{Cite web|Japanese book|author= |date= |url=http://www.city.fujisawa.kanagawa.jp/kouhou/khf100310/sisei02.html |title=Enriching the region through location support! ~Shonan Fujisawa Film Commission |publisher=Fujisawa City |accessdate=2018-11-10}}. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=543&cd=1206024&scd=&npg=0 Oba Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) === Kumano Shrine (Oba, Fujisawa City) === {{Shrine |Name = Kumano Shrine |Image= [[File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma03.JPG|250px]]
Oratory
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E}}}} |Location = 1846 Oba, Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|scale:5000|name=Kumano Shrine (Oba Shrine)}} |Enshrined deity = Kumano Kusuhi no Mikoto |Shakaku = Shikinaisha (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alias = Gongen-sama |Regular festival = September 9th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}[] ``Kumano Shrine'' is a shrine located in Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. In the Edo period, it was called ``Goryosha'' and ``Gongensha,'' but stone monuments and information boards in the precincts say ``Oba Shrine 舊达''. , or "Oba Shrine Historic Site" [https://web.archive.org/web/20151130003000/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/sagami/bun/sag160501-02.html Kumano Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines). === Enshrined God === * '''Kumano Kusuhime''' === Historic site of Oba Shrine and Obago === This area is said to be the historic site of Oba Shrine. From the hilly area in front of this area, you can see {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} from the early {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} period, {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{jalin} k|Nara Period|Q189178}} Remains of residences have been found ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oba Tsukiyama Ruins|1=UNKNOWN}}), and it is assumed that the {{ill|lt=village|Q486972|WD=Q486972|1=Human settlement}} of Oba-go was formed from ancient times.. === History === Similar to Oba Shrine, an Inari shrine in the same city (see {{ill|lt=#Oba Shrine (Fujisawa City Inari)|UNKNOWN|lt=previous section}}), there are no records (shrine biographies) from before modern times. It was during the Edo period that our company became the historic site of Oba Shrine (Oba Shrine). name="chosa2" />. The aforementioned ``Fujisawa Cultural Properties'' discusses that our company was originally ``Oba Shrine,'' an Engishiki shrine, but that for some reason, Oba Shrine in Inari came to be regarded as an Engishiki shrine . On the other hand, it is also said that it was {{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q18989808|WD=Q18989808|1=Bunsha}}) from {{ill|lt=Goryo Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa City)|Q20044399|WD=Q20044399|lt=Goryo Shrine|1=Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)}} located in Miyamae, Fujisawa City. In {{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}4 ({{ill|lt=1833|Q7587|WD=Q7587|1=1833}}), a stone torii gate was donated by Rokubei Masahiko Yamazaki. Furthermore, this shrine was also managed by Joju-in until the Meiji era's ordinance separating Shinto and Buddhism. File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma01.JPG|
Stone Torii

Oratory
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}Inari997 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|scale:5000}} |Enshrined deity = God of the Emperor |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Tenjin-sama |Regular festival = September 15th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Oba Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}'''Oba Shrine'' (Oba Shrine) is {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} {{jalink|Sagami Province|Q105 described in "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) 3732}} is one (small shrine) of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|lt=13 Shikinaisha in Engi|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}}, but there are two {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (Hijosha). The distance between the two companies is relatively close at approximately 1.5{{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|lt=km|1=Kilometre}} (about 20 minutes walk). Both companies are listed in this section. === Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City) === '''Oba Shrine'' is located near the border with {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Inari {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. It is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} that is enshrined in . Located on {{ill|lt=hills|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} along {{ill|lt=Hikiji River|Q11487144|WD=Q11487144|1=Hikiji River}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Tenjingu," "Oba Daimyojin," and "Oba Tenmangu." Oba Shrine] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine). === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinnobureishin|1=Kamimusubi}}''' ({{ill|lt=Enshrined Deity#Main Deity/Augami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deity|1=Saijin}}) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Kagechika Oba|Q1155414|WD=Q1155414|lt=Kagechika Saburo Oba|1=Ōba Kagechika}} === History === Although "Engishiki Jinmyocho" compiled in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} mentions "{{ill|lt=Koza District|Q1202776|WD=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}} Oba Shrine," there are no other records (shaden) prior to {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}. The information board in the precincts about our company's history says, ``It was ranked as one of the 13 Sagami shrines and is a small shrine, and this place has been passed down as an old place since ancient times.'' "Sagami 13-no-Ichi Nishite-kosha 2 rows of Serarere are the old folklore of ancient places". The {{ill|lt=Bōshu bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} in the temple grounds was built in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}}6 ({{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}}). alink|casting|Q496098}}, and it is said that the words ``Tenmatenjingu'' were originally written on it, but these words were scratched off and the words ``Oba Daimyojin'' were carved into it. Also, according to "Fujisawa Cultural Properties" published by the {{ill|lt=Board of Education|Q399541|WD=Q399541|1=Board of education}} of Fujisawa City, our company was called "Tenjin Shrine" before {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}[http://pub.ne.jp/karihomaru/?entry_id=3609822 Two Oba Shrines] (Shonan Mark's room).{{ill|lt=Yasunaga (era name)|Q1193910|WD=Q1193910|lt=Yasunaga|1=An'ei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1777|Q7709|WD=Q7709|1=1777}}), King Shien of Jingi acquired Saburo Kagechika Oba, who was based in {{ill|lt=Oba Castle|Q11435376|WD=Q11435376|1=Oba Castle}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenmei|1=UNKNOWN}}3 ({{ill|lt=1783|Q7745|WD=Q7745|1=1783}}), Michima Sugawara was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}) according to the wishes of Suwabe Sadaro, Yamazaki Rokurobei Kanataka, and others. Before the Meiji era {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ordinance on the Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Jōjuin (Fujisawa City)|Q18337056|WD=Q18337056|lt=Jōjuin|1=Jōjuin}}, located behind our company, was managing our company as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. File:Ōba-jinja 01.jpg|
{{ill|lt=Oratory|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} (July 2010)
File:OOBA-jinjya-ten02.JPG|
Temple bell
File:OOBA-jinjya-ten03.JPG|{{ill|lt=Great Kanto Earthquake|Q3742341|WD=Q3742341|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake}} Remains of {{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} === Transportation === {{osm box|n|1229000716|Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City)}} * From {{ill|lt=Fujisawa Station|Q1133110|WD=Q1133110|1=Fujisawa Station}} North Exit/{{ill|lt=Shonandai Station|Q1036357|WD=Q1036357|1=Shōnandai Station}} West Exit {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|Q10854960|WD=Q10854960|lt=Kanachu Bus|1=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu}}{{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu Fujisawa Office #Fujisawa Station North Exit - Ishikawa Bridge - Isshikigami - From the west exit of Shonandai Station|Q11588895|WD=Q11588895|lt=Fuji 34|1=Fuji 34}}, or from the west exit of Zengyo Station, take the Kanachu bus {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu Fujisawa Office #Zengyo Station - Wholesale Market Front|Q11588895|WD=Q11588895|lt=Zen 03|1=Zen 03}}, get off at Tenjinsha-mae, and walk 6 minutes to Ichi-no-Torii. * About 25 minutes walk from Odakyu Line {{ill|lt=Zengyo Station|Q4160904|WD=Q4160904|1=Zengyō Station}}. === A work set at Oba Shrine === * {{ill|lt=Futatsu no Spica|Q2511557|WD=Q2511557|1=Twin Spica}} - In {{ill|lt=2009|Q1996|WD=Q1996|1=2009}}, location shooting for the TV drama version that was broadcast in the "{{ill|lt=Drama 8|Q11323102|WD=Q11323102|1=Drama 8}}" slot of {{ill|lt=NHK General Television|Q909336|WD=Q909336|lt=NHK General Television|1=NHK General TV}} was held{{Cite web|Japanese book|author= |date= |url=http://www.city.fujisawa.kanagawa.jp/kouhou/khf100310/sisei02.html |title=Enriching the region through location support! ~Shonan Fujisawa Film Commission |publisher=Fujisawa City |accessdate=2018-11-10}}. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=543&cd=1206024&scd=&npg=0 Oba Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) === Kumano Shrine (Oba, Fujisawa City) === {{Shrine |Name = Kumano Shrine |Image= [[File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma03.JPG|250px]]
Oratory
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E}}}} |Location = 1846 Oba, Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|scale:5000|name=Kumano Shrine (Oba Shrine)}} |Enshrined deity = Kumano Kusuhi no Mikoto |Shakaku = Shikinaisha (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alias = Gongen-sama |Regular festival = September 9th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}[] ``Kumano Shrine'' is a shrine located in Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. In the Edo period, it was called ``Goryosha'' and ``Gongensha,'' but stone monuments and information boards in the precincts say ``Oba Shrine 舊达''. , or "Oba Shrine Historic Site" [https://web.archive.org/web/20151130003000/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/sagami/bun/sag160501-02.html Kumano Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines). === Enshrined God === * '''Kumano Kusuhime''' === Historic site of Oba Shrine and Obago === This area is said to be the historic site of Oba Shrine. From the hilly area in front of this area, you can see {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} from the early {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} period, {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{jalin} k|Nara Period|Q189178}} Remains of residences have been found ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oba Tsukiyama Ruins|1=UNKNOWN}}), and it is assumed that the {{ill|lt=village|Q486972|WD=Q486972|1=Human settlement}} of Oba-go was formed from ancient times.. === History === Similar to Oba Shrine, an Inari shrine in the same city (see {{ill|lt=#Oba Shrine (Fujisawa City Inari)|UNKNOWN|lt=previous section}}), there are no records (shrine biographies) from before modern times. It was during the Edo period that our company became the historic site of Oba Shrine (Oba Shrine). name="chosa2" />. The aforementioned ``Fujisawa Cultural Properties'' discusses that our company was originally ``Oba Shrine,'' an Engishiki shrine, but that for some reason, Oba Shrine in Inari came to be regarded as an Engishiki shrine . On the other hand, it is also said that it was {{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q18989808|WD=Q18989808|1=Bunsha}}) from {{ill|lt=Goryo Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa City)|Q20044399|WD=Q20044399|lt=Goryo Shrine|1=Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)}} located in Miyamae, Fujisawa City. In {{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}4 ({{ill|lt=1833|Q7587|WD=Q7587|1=1833}}), a stone torii gate was donated by Rokubei Masahiko Yamazaki. Furthermore, this shrine was also managed by Joju-in until the Meiji era's ordinance separating Shinto and Buddhism. File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma01.JPG|
Stone Torii
File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma04.JPG|
{{ill|lt=approach|approach|WD=approach|ltq=Q667783}}
File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma05.JPG|
Rotten information board
 === Transportation === * Take the Kanachu Bus Fuji 34 from the north exit of Fujisawa Station or the west exit of Shonandai Station, or the Kanachu Bus Zen 03 from the west exit of Yoshiyuki Station, get off at "Benten-mae" and walk 6 minutes. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=545&cd=1206026&scd=&npg=0 Kumano Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Agency) === Notes === Currently, {{ill|lt=Kodai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|Q11675783|WD=Q11675783|lt=Kodai Jingu|1=Kōdai-jingū}} is in charge of managing both shrines, so you will need to get the {{ill|lt=Red seal (shrines and temples)|Q7504772|WD=Q7504772|lt=Goshuin|1=Shuin}} from there. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Annotations"}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * From the precincts information board of Oba Shrine and Kumano Shrine === Related items === {{Commonscat|Ooba-jinja (Shikinaisya)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Sagami country|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|Q11588941|WD=Q11588941|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mikuriya Oba|Q11435381|WD=Q11435381|1=Mikuriya Oba}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:相模国の式内社]] [[Category:大庭氏|社]] [[Category:pages]]

Located on {{ill|lt=hills|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} along {{ill|lt=Hikiji River|Q11487144|WD=Q11487144|1=Hikiji River}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Tenjingu," "Oba Daimyojin," and "Oba Tenmangu." Oba Shrine] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine). === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinnobureishin|1=Kamimusubi}}''' ({{ill|lt=Enshrined Deity#Main Deity/Augami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deity|1=Saijin}}) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Kagechika Oba|Q1155414|WD=Q1155414|lt=Kagechika Saburo Oba|1=Ōba Kagechika}} === History === Although "Engishiki Jinmyocho" compiled in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} mentions "{{ill|lt=Koza District|Q1202776|WD=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}} Oba Shrine," there are no other records (shaden) prior to {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}. The information board in the precincts about our company's history says, ``It was ranked as one of the 13 Sagami shrines and is a small shrine, and this place has been passed down as an old place since ancient times.'' "Sagami 13-no-Ichi Nishite-kosha 2 rows of Serarere are the old folklore of ancient places". The {{ill|lt=Bōshu bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} in the temple grounds was built in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}}6 ({{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}}). alink|casting|Q496098}}, and it is said that the words ``Tenmatenjingu'' were originally written on it, but these words were scratched off and the words ``Oba Daimyojin'' were carved into it. Also, according to "Fujisawa Cultural Properties" published by the {{ill|lt=Board of Education|Q399541|WD=Q399541|1=Board of education}} of Fujisawa City, our company was called "Tenjin Shrine" before {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}[http://pub.ne.jp/karihomaru/?entry_id=3609822 Two Oba Shrines] (Shonan Mark's room).{{ill|lt=Yasunaga (era name)|Q1193910|WD=Q1193910|lt=Yasunaga|1=An'ei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1777|Q7709|WD=Q7709|1=1777}}), King Shien of Jingi acquired Saburo Kagechika Oba, who was based in {{ill|lt=Oba Castle|Q11435376|WD=Q11435376|1=Oba Castle}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenmei|1=UNKNOWN}}3 ({{ill|lt=1783|Q7745|WD=Q7745|1=1783}}), Michima Sugawara was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}) according to the wishes of Suwabe Sadaro, Yamazaki Rokurobei Kanataka, and others. Before the Meiji era {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ordinance on the Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Jōjuin (Fujisawa City)|Q18337056|WD=Q18337056|lt=Jōjuin|1=Jōjuin}}, located behind our company, was managing our company as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. File:Ōba-jinja 01.jpg|
{{ill|lt=Oratory|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} (July 2010)

Temple bell
File:OOBA-jinjya-ten03.JPG|{{ill|lt=Great Kanto Earthquake|Q3742341|WD=Q3742341|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake}} Remains of {{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} === Transportation === {{osm box|n|1229000716|Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City)}} * From {{ill|lt=Fujisawa Station|Q1133110|WD=Q1133110|1=Fujisawa Station}} North Exit/{{ill|lt=Shonandai Station|Q1036357|WD=Q1036357|1=Shōnandai Station}} West Exit {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|Q10854960|WD=Q10854960|lt=Kanachu Bus|1=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu}}{{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu Fujisawa Office #Fujisawa Station North Exit - Ishikawa Bridge - Isshikigami - From the west exit of Shonandai Station|Q11588895|WD=Q11588895|lt=Fuji 34|1=Fuji 34}}, or from the west exit of Zengyo Station, take the Kanachu bus {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu Fujisawa Office #Zengyo Station - Wholesale Market Front|Q11588895|WD=Q11588895|lt=Zen 03|1=Zen 03}}, get off at Tenjinsha-mae, and walk 6 minutes to Ichi-no-Torii. * About 25 minutes walk from Odakyu Line {{ill|lt=Zengyo Station|Q4160904|WD=Q4160904|1=Zengyō Station}}. === A work set at Oba Shrine === * {{ill|lt=Futatsu no Spica|Q2511557|WD=Q2511557|1=Twin Spica}} - In {{ill|lt=2009|Q1996|WD=Q1996|1=2009}}, location shooting for the TV drama version that was broadcast in the "{{ill|lt=Drama 8|Q11323102|WD=Q11323102|1=Drama 8}}" slot of {{ill|lt=NHK General Television|Q909336|WD=Q909336|lt=NHK General Television|1=NHK General TV}} was held{{Cite web|Japanese book|author= |date= |url=http://www.city.fujisawa.kanagawa.jp/kouhou/khf100310/sisei02.html |title=Enriching the region through location support! ~Shonan Fujisawa Film Commission |publisher=Fujisawa City |accessdate=2018-11-10}}. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=543&cd=1206024&scd=&npg=0 Oba Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) === Kumano Shrine (Oba, Fujisawa City) === {{Shrine |Name = Kumano Shrine |Image= [[File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma03.JPG|250px]]
Oratory
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E}}}} |Location = 1846 Oba, Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|scale:5000|name=Kumano Shrine (Oba Shrine)}} |Enshrined deity = Kumano Kusuhi no Mikoto |Shakaku = Shikinaisha (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alias = Gongen-sama |Regular festival = September 9th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}[] ``Kumano Shrine'' is a shrine located in Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. In the Edo period, it was called ``Goryosha'' and ``Gongensha,'' but stone monuments and information boards in the precincts say ``Oba Shrine 舊达''. , or "Oba Shrine Historic Site" [https://web.archive.org/web/20151130003000/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/sagami/bun/sag160501-02.html Kumano Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines). === Enshrined God === * '''Kumano Kusuhime''' === Historic site of Oba Shrine and Obago === This area is said to be the historic site of Oba Shrine. From the hilly area in front of this area, you can see {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} from the early {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} period, {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{jalin} k|Nara Period|Q189178}} Remains of residences have been found ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oba Tsukiyama Ruins|1=UNKNOWN}}), and it is assumed that the {{ill|lt=village|Q486972|WD=Q486972|1=Human settlement}} of Oba-go was formed from ancient times.. === History === Similar to Oba Shrine, an Inari shrine in the same city (see {{ill|lt=#Oba Shrine (Fujisawa City Inari)|UNKNOWN|lt=previous section}}), there are no records (shrine biographies) from before modern times. It was during the Edo period that our company became the historic site of Oba Shrine (Oba Shrine). name="chosa2" />. The aforementioned ``Fujisawa Cultural Properties'' discusses that our company was originally ``Oba Shrine,'' an Engishiki shrine, but that for some reason, Oba Shrine in Inari came to be regarded as an Engishiki shrine . On the other hand, it is also said that it was {{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q18989808|WD=Q18989808|1=Bunsha}}) from {{ill|lt=Goryo Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa City)|Q20044399|WD=Q20044399|lt=Goryo Shrine|1=Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)}} located in Miyamae, Fujisawa City. In {{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}4 ({{ill|lt=1833|Q7587|WD=Q7587|1=1833}}), a stone torii gate was donated by Rokubei Masahiko Yamazaki. Furthermore, this shrine was also managed by Joju-in until the Meiji era's ordinance separating Shinto and Buddhism. File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma01.JPG|
Stone Torii

 === Transportation === * Take the Kanachu Bus Fuji 34 from the north exit of Fujisawa Station or the west exit of Shonandai Station, or the Kanachu Bus Zen 03 from the west exit of Yoshiyuki Station, get off at "Benten-mae" and walk 6 minutes. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=545&cd=1206026&scd=&npg=0 Kumano Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Agency) === Notes === Currently, {{ill|lt=Kodai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|Q11675783|WD=Q11675783|lt=Kodai Jingu|1=Kōdai-jingū}} is in charge of managing both shrines, so you will need to get the {{ill|lt=Red seal (shrines and temples)|Q7504772|WD=Q7504772|lt=Goshuin|1=Shuin}} from there. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Annotations"}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * From the precincts information board of Oba Shrine and Kumano Shrine === Related items === {{Commonscat|Ooba-jinja (Shikinaisya)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Sagami country|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|Q11588941|WD=Q11588941|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mikuriya Oba|Q11435381|WD=Q11435381|1=Mikuriya Oba}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:相模国の式内社]] [[Category:Oba Clan]] [[Category:pages]]

The distance between the two companies is relatively close at approximately 1.5{{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|lt=km|1=Kilometre}} (about 20 minutes walk). Both companies are listed in this section. === Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City) === '''Oba Shrine'' is located near the border with {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Inari {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. It is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} that is enshrined in . Located on {{ill|lt=hills|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} along {{ill|lt=Hikiji River|Q11487144|WD=Q11487144|1=Hikiji River}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Tenjingu," "Oba Daimyojin," and "Oba Tenmangu." ~Shonan Fujisawa Film Commission |publisher=Fujisawa City |accessdate=2018-11-10}}. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=543&cd=1206024&scd=&npg=0 Oba Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Shrine Agency|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) === Kumano Shrine (Oba, Fujisawa City) === {{Shrine |Name = Kumano Shrine |Image= [[File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma03.JPG|250px]]
Oratory
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E}}}} |Location = 1846 Oba, Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|scale:5000|name=Kumano Shrine (Oba Shrine)}} |Enshrined deity = Kumano Kusuhi no Mikoto |Shakaku = Shikinaisha (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alias = Gongen-sama |Regular festival = September 9th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|15.9|N|139|27|6.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}[] ``Kumano Shrine'' is a shrine located in Fujisawa City, Kanagawa Prefecture {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. In the Edo period, it was called ``Goryosha'' and ``Gongensha,'' but stone monuments and information boards in the precincts say ``Oba Shrine 舊达''. , or "Oba Shrine Historic Site" [https://web.archive.org/web/20151130003000/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/sagami/bun/sag160501-02.html Kumano Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines). === Enshrined God === * '''Kumano Kusuhime''' === Historic site of Oba Shrine and Obago === This area is said to be the historic site of Oba Shrine. From the hilly area in front of this area, you can see {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} from the early {{ill|lt=Jomon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} period, {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{jalin} k|Nara Period|Q189178}} Remains of residences have been found ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oba Tsukiyama Ruins|1=UNKNOWN}}), and it is assumed that the {{ill|lt=village|Q486972|WD=Q486972|1=Human settlement}} of Oba-go was formed from ancient times.. === History === Similar to Oba Shrine, an Inari shrine in the same city (see {{ill|lt=#Oba Shrine (Fujisawa City Inari)|UNKNOWN|lt=previous section}}), there are no records (shrine biographies) from before modern times. It was during the Edo period that our company became the historic site of Oba Shrine (Oba Shrine). name="chosa2" />. The aforementioned ``Fujisawa Cultural Properties'' discusses that our company was originally ``Oba Shrine,'' an Engishiki shrine, but that for some reason, Oba Shrine in Inari came to be regarded as an Engishiki shrine . On the other hand, it is also said that it was {{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q18989808|WD=Q18989808|1=Bunsha}}) from {{ill|lt=Goryo Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa City)|Q20044399|WD=Q20044399|lt=Goryo Shrine|1=Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)}} located in Miyamae, Fujisawa City. In {{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}4 ({{ill|lt=1833|Q7587|WD=Q7587|1=1833}}), a stone torii gate was donated by Rokubei Masahiko Yamazaki. Furthermore, this shrine was also managed by Joju-in until the Meiji era's ordinance separating Shinto and Buddhism. File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma01.JPG|

 === Transportation === * Take the Kanachu Bus Fuji 34 from the north exit of Fujisawa Station or the west exit of Shonandai Station, or the Kanachu Bus Zen 03 from the west exit of Yoshiyuki Station, get off at "Benten-mae" and walk 6 minutes. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=545&cd=1206026&scd=&npg=0 Kumano Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Agency) === Notes === Currently, {{ill|lt=Kodai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|Q11675783|WD=Q11675783|lt=Kodai Jingu|1=Kōdai-jingū}} is in charge of managing both shrines, so you will need to get the {{ill|lt=Red seal (shrines and temples)|Q7504772|WD=Q7504772|lt=Goshuin|1=Shuin}} from there. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Annotations"}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * From the precincts information board of Oba Shrine and Kumano Shrine === Related items === {{Commonscat|Ooba-jinja (Shikinaisya)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Sagami country|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|Q11588941|WD=Q11588941|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mikuriya Oba|Q11435381|WD=Q11435381|1=Mikuriya Oba}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:Oba Clan]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}Inari997 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|scale:5000}} |Enshrined deity = God of the Emperor |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Tenjin-sama |Regular festival = September 15th |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Oba Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|37.5|N|139|27|30.5|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }}'''Oba Shrine'' (Oba Shrine) is {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} {{jalink|Sagami Province|Q105 described in "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) 3732}} is one (small shrine) of the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|lt=13 Shikinaisha in Engi|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}}, but there are two {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (Hijosha). The distance between the two companies is relatively close at approximately 1.5{{ill|lt=kilometer|Q828224|WD=Q828224|lt=km|1=Kilometre}} (about 20 minutes walk). Both companies are listed in this section. === Oba Shrine (Inari, Fujisawa City) === '''Oba Shrine'' is located near the border with {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujisawa City|Q386661|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Inari {{ill|lt=Oba (Fujisawa City)|Q11435364|WD=Q11435364|lt=Oba|1=Ōba}}. It is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} that is enshrined in . Located on {{ill|lt=hills|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}} along {{ill|lt=Hikiji River|Q11487144|WD=Q11487144|1=Hikiji River}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called "Tenjingu," "Oba Daimyojin," and "Oba Tenmangu." Oba Shrine] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine). === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinnobureishin|1=Kamimusubi}}''' ({{ill|lt=Enshrined Deity#Main Deity/Augami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main Enshrined Deity|1=Saijin}}) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Kagechika Oba|Q1155414|WD=Q1155414|lt=Kagechika Saburo Oba|1=Ōba Kagechika}} === History === Although "Engishiki Jinmyocho" compiled in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} mentions "{{ill|lt=Koza District|Q1202776|WD=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}} Oba Shrine," there are no other records (shaden) prior to {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}. The information board in the precincts about our company's history says, ``It was ranked as one of the 13 Sagami shrines and is a small shrine, and this place has been passed down as an old place since ancient times.'' "Sagami 13-no-Ichi Nishite-kosha 2 rows of Serarere are the old folklore of ancient places". The {{ill|lt=Bōshu bell|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} in the temple grounds was built in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}}6 ({{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}}). alink|casting|Q496098}}, and it is said that the words ``Tenmatenjingu'' were originally written on it, but these words were scratched off and the words ``Oba Daimyojin'' were carved into it. Also, according to "Fujisawa Cultural Properties" published by the {{ill|lt=Board of Education|Q399541|WD=Q399541|1=Board of education}} of Fujisawa City, our company was called "Tenjin Shrine" before {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}[http://pub.ne.jp/karihomaru/?entry_id=3609822 Two Oba Shrines] (Shonan Mark's room).{{ill|lt=Yasunaga (era name)|Q1193910|WD=Q1193910|lt=Yasunaga|1=An'ei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1777|Q7709|WD=Q7709|1=1777}}), King Shien of Jingi acquired Saburo Kagechika Oba, who was based in {{ill|lt=Oba Castle|Q11435376|WD=Q11435376|1=Oba Castle}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenmei|1=UNKNOWN}}3 ({{ill|lt=1783|Q7745|WD=Q7745|1=1783}}), Michima Sugawara was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}) according to the wishes of Suwabe Sadaro, Yamazaki Rokurobei Kanataka, and others. Before the Meiji era {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ordinance on the Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Jōjuin (Fujisawa City)|Q18337056|WD=Q18337056|lt=Jōjuin|1=Jōjuin}}, located behind our company, was managing our company as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. The aforementioned ``Fujisawa Cultural Properties'' discusses that our company was originally ``Oba Shrine,'' an Engishiki shrine, but that for some reason, Oba Shrine in Inari came to be regarded as an Engishiki shrine . On the other hand, it is also said that it was {{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q195793|WD=Q195793|1=Bunrei}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsha|Q18989808|WD=Q18989808|1=Bunsha}}) from {{ill|lt=Goryo Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa City)|Q20044399|WD=Q20044399|lt=Goryo Shrine|1=Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)}} located in Miyamae, Fujisawa City. In {{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}4 ({{ill|lt=1833|Q7587|WD=Q7587|1=1833}}), a stone torii gate was donated by Rokubei Masahiko Yamazaki. Furthermore, this shrine was also managed by Joju-in until the Meiji era's ordinance separating Shinto and Buddhism. File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma01.JPG|

 === Transportation === * Take the Kanachu Bus Fuji 34 from the north exit of Fujisawa Station or the west exit of Shonandai Station, or the Kanachu Bus Zen 03 from the west exit of Yoshiyuki Station, get off at "Benten-mae" and walk 6 minutes. === External link === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=545&cd=1206026&scd=&npg=0 Kumano Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Shrine Agency) === Notes === Currently, {{ill|lt=Kodai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|Q11675783|WD=Q11675783|lt=Kodai Jingu|1=Kōdai-jingū}} is in charge of managing both shrines, so you will need to get the {{ill|lt=Red seal (shrines and temples)|Q7504772|WD=Q7504772|lt=Goshuin|1=Shuin}} from there. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Annotations"}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * From the precincts information board of Oba Shrine and Kumano Shrine === Related items === {{Commonscat|Ooba-jinja (Shikinaisya)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Sagami country|Q11582137|WD=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|Q11588941|WD=Q11588941|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mikuriya Oba|Q11435381|WD=Q11435381|1=Mikuriya Oba}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ooba Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sagami Province]] [[Category:Oba Clan]]
